LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF CHARAN SIJHER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: CONTINUED TO MAY 2, 2007. OPPOSING PARTY TO FILE OPPOSITION. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

LAW AND MOTION, 302, THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. AFTER THE ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF CHARAN SIJHER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED BECAUSE THE MOTION FAILS TO ADDRESS EACH CAUSE OF ACTION. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED BECAUSE PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE THAT PLAINTIFF HAS OR CAN RESTORE TO DEFENDANT EVERYTHING OF VALUE THAT PLAINTIFF RECEIVED(CCP 1691(B)). PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE AN ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

Default Prove-Up. (Default Entered Only As To One Defendant: Seth)
Off Calendar Jul-05-2007 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled for Default Prove-Up Hearing this day.
Dept. 218, Comm. Sue Kaplan, Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha, CSR: Susan Lee.
Melisa McCoy Evans, Esq. for Plaintiff: Charan S. Sijher, also present, was sworn and direct examined.
After hearing the tetimony and evidence presented, the Court rules in favor of the Plaintiff and against the Defaulted Defendants: Bipin Seth dba the Estate Group and Indus Valley Group as follows:
Principal $275,000.00
Prejudgment Interest $64,875.00
Costs $340.32, for a total of $340,215.32
Counsel to prepare a Proposed Judgment.
July 5, 2007
Proposed Judgment rec'd in court, granted and signed by Comm. Kaplan.
